title: Light Garlicky Spanish Garlic Paella
description: A light and festive Spanish seafood paella dish featuring rose water, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This gluten-free-friendly paella is packed with the flavors of the Mediterranean.

ingredients.name: Extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.notes: High-quality, cold-pressed oil for added flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Use a mix of sweet and pungent garlic varieties for depth.

ingredients.name: Rose water
ingredients.notes: Add a floral touch to balance the richness.

ingredients.name: Seafood mix
ingredients.notes: Combine shrimp, mussels, clams, and squid for a seafood lover's delight.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoky, rich flavor for added depth.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use a flaky sea salt for best flavor.

ingredients.name: Pepper
ingredients.notes: Use a coarse black pepper for added texture.

ingredients.name: Saffron threads
ingredients.notes: Use high-quality saffron for maximum flavor.

instructions: Combine the rice and water in a large mixing bowl and let it soak for 30 minutes.
Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan or skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the garlic and saut and stir until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Add the seafood mix and cook until it starts to turn pink, about 2 minutes.
Add the artichoke hearts, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the soaked rice mixture and stir to combine.
Add the tagine cook liquid and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and cover the pan with a tight-fitting lid.
Simmer the paella for 20-25 minutes or until the rice is tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Stir in the rose water, saffron, and chopped parsley and lemon for garnish.
Serve the paella hot, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
